{"query":"Berkshire Hathaway Kraft Heinz stake sale 2026 latest transaction value","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1737,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/2026/01/24/new-berkshire-ceo-abel-quickly-signals-troubled-kraft-heinz-stake-could-be-toast.html","title":"New Berkshire CEO Abel quickly signals troubled Kraft Heinz stake could be toast","content":"An SEC filing after Tuesday's closing bell by Kraft Heinz says Berkshire, by far its largest shareholder with a 27.5% stake currently worth $7.5 billion, may sell "up to" almost all its shares.

Kraft Heinz's shares fell to ​as low as $21.99 on Wednesday.
Berkshire under Warren Buffett, and now led by Greg Abel, along with 3G Capital, was instrumental in the 2015 merger that ‍formed Kraft Heinz.
But its investment has been marked by a $3 billion writedown in 2019 and another $3.76 billion last year.
Kraft Heinz appointed industry veteran Steve Cahillane as its new CEO ​starting in January."},{"url":"https://www.investing.com/analysis/as-berkshire-exits-its-kraft-heinz-position-is-the-stock-a-sell-200674021","title":"As Berkshire Exits Its Kraft Heinz Position, Is the Stock a Sell? | Investing.com","content":"Last week, it was reported that newly instated Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRKb) CEO Greg Abel has initiated the process to sell the company’s nearly 28% stake—or approximately 325 million shares—in consumer staples giant Kraft Heinz (NASDAQ:KHC).
The move, which occurred less than one month after Abel took the reins from predecessor Warren Buffett, comes in the wake of KHC shares kicking off the year by losing more than 3%, following a 2025 performance that saw the stock slide by more than 21%.
In September 2025, Kraft Heinz announced that it will be splitting into two scaled, focused independent companies.
That division into two entities—tentatively named Global Taste Elevation Co. and North American Grocery Co.—will be finalized in the second half of 2026.
Currently, Kraft Heinz’s dividend yields an attractive 6.59%, or $1.60 per share annually.
Of the 23 analysts currently covering the stock, only one assigns it a Buy rating, with 17 assigning it a Hold, and five assigning it a Sell.
Institutional ownership remains strong at more than 78%, but that figure is likely to drop once Berkshire Hathaway completes its sale of KHC shares."},{"url":"https://www.coinglass.com/stocks/KHC","title":"Kraft Foods (KHC) Stock Price Today, Pre-Market & After- ...","date":"2 days ago","content":"2 days ago — Berkshire Hathaway's new CEO Greg Abel may be preparing to liquidate the company's $7.3 billion stake in Kraft Heinz, one of Warren Buffett's ...Read more"}]}

Kraft Heinz (NASDAQ: KHC) opened in the red this morning after Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE: BRK.B) registered its entire stake (27.5%) in the packaged foods spe...","score":0.85186684,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/04/28/kraft-heinz-was-a-rare-buffett-blunder-now-berkshi","title":"Kraft Heinz Was a Rare Buffett Blunder. Now Berkshire's New Boss Looks Ready to Move On. | The Motley Fool","content":"**Kraft Heinz** (KHC +0.69%) was one of the Oracle of Omaha's errors. In what was supposed to be a \"dream partnership,\" Berkshire teamed up with Brazil's 3G Capital in 2013 to orchestrate the $55 billion merger of Kraft and Heinz, creating what was then one of the largest consumer staples companies in the world. That transaction made Buffett's conglomerate the largest shareholder in Kraft Heinz. It's no wonder Greg Abel, the new CEO of Berkshire, pondered liquidating the company's Kraft Heinz stake. On the more positive side of the coin, Abel clearly stated that, now that Kraft Heinz has ditched the breakup proposal, Berkshire doesn't plan to sell any or all of its equity in the ketchup maker. With $373 billion in cash and equivalents on its booksas of the end of 2025, Berkshire doesn't need the liquidity it would free up by selling Kraft shares, but all that says is that the conglomerate has the luxury of holding on to the stock.","score":0.7522497,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://finance.yahoo.com/news/berkshire-hathaway-brk-potential-kraft-020923930.html","title":"What Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A)'s Potential Kraft Heinz Exit Means For Shareholders","content":"3. Best money market accounts. # What Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A)'s Potential Kraft Heinz Exit Means For Shareholders. * Earlier in January 2026, Kraft Heinz disclosed in an SEC filing that Berkshire Hathaway, now led by CEO Greg Abel, may sell up to its entire 325,442,152-share stake, potentially ending a decade-long investment Warren Buffett helped create through the 2015 Kraft–Heinz merger. * This possible full exit marks a rare reversal of a high-profile Buffett-era deal and offers an early signal of how Abel may reshape Berkshire's portfolio and capital allocation priorities. * We'll now examine how this potential divestment of the Kraft Heinz stake could influence Berkshire Hathaway's investment narrative under Greg Abel's leadership. ## What Is Berkshire Hathaway's Investment Narrative?
